reviews.review_description:
Environments are really good. Very atmospheric and serene.

As much as it feels like a meditation app, I'd say this would also serve as a technical showpiece of what the quest 3 is capable of. 

When you "spawn" in, there's a room to the left that lets you adjust super sampling and anti aliasing. I'd recommend doing that before starting, as it will look a bit blurry / grainy if not

This isn't much of a game but more of an experience. The environments look fantastic and the sound effects are great, there's a little exploration.

The only experience I didn't enjoy as much was the snow environment I feel like it could have been great with a mini cabin with a crackling fire inside and a light snowfall with a breeze.

Other than that I really enjoyed myself and hope to see future environments keep up the good work!

This app has the best water physics on Quest (comparable to PCVR). The scenes are as beautiful as depicted in the trailer. It’s not so much a game, but more of an immersive experience where you can feel like you’re on vacation and just relax. Pro tip: you can open your web browser while in the app and watch Netflix, YouTube, read books, etc. while enjoying the beautiful environments in this app. 

Recommendations: 
1. It would be great there was haptic feedback, especially when interacting with the water.

2. This might be asking a lot, but it would be awesome if there were a mixed reality mode where you can draw windows in your real room and be able to see these virtual environments through the windows. (Like in Ocean Rift).

developer_response.body:
Thank you, I didn't know the web browser thing.
There is a haptic feedback, but not for the water.

reviews.review_description:
beautiful relaxing experience 

Top tip ..on the underwater level switch on swim mode ..breast stroke through the level to the surface ...take a breath and dive with the sharks & whales until u run out of breath then surface again & breath ...ultimate immersion & so beautifully made also terrifying when u surface & realise your alone treading water in the middle of the ocean 

Cheers

reviews.review_title: Make more maps as a dlc but good job
reviews.review_description: For an idea make a fall season going down a path or waterfall in a forest or sakura trees. In japan,the game looks amazing and very very detailed i hope it gets updated for more maps but great job

reviews.review_title: Cool but just dete demo not worth 10
reviews.review_description:
This game is interesting for the first few minutes, but it quickly starts to feel more like a basic tech demo than a full experience. The water physics are decent and fun to mess with at first, but there’s not much you can actually do with them besides splashing around. You also can’t swim, which feels like a missed opportunity for a game focused so heavily on water physics.

The game advertises itself as realistic, but the visuals still feel fairly pixelated and the shadows look flat and artificial. A lot of the environments seem like they should be interactive, but most of them really aren’t. In the house level, for example, you can’t flush the toilet, use the sink, or interact with many objects in meaningful ways. Throwing items out the window just makes them float outside awkwardly. Even smaller details, like rebuilding the snowman or moving grass, aren’t possible.

The fish AI is also disappointing. If you move the fish onto land, they just sit there and do nothing, which breaks immersion pretty quickly.

The later levels feel even weaker than the earlier ones. The moon level sounds exciting at first, but there’s barely anything to do there. There’s no real zero-gravity gameplay, and since movement options are limited, the environment ends up feeling empty and unfinished.

Overall, the game has some potential, especially with the physics and environments, but right now there just isn’t enough content or interaction to keep it interesting. Most levels feel like something you look at for a few minutes before moving on. For the current amount of content, $10 feels overpriced — it would make more sense as a free experience or maybe around $5. Honestly, exploring interactive VR worlds In for example vrc can provide a more engaging experience with similar physics features.

Graphics to rivalry PC VR. Absolutely best graphics I've ever seen on meta quest, this experience is simply the best the meta quest will have to offer in graphics and sound quality for years.  I've been playing vr since 2016 and never seen such beautiful graphics,  the water Physics are just outstanding. To the bad reviews people need to know this is not a game,  it's a relax and explore  vr experience, and I haven't had any problems, and understood its not a game,  but that being said I've never had so much fun playing with sticks and coconuts in the water. Absolutely brilliant 👏 👌 😀 👍

Wow , the water effects are so realistic and the soundscapes are unreal , truly immersive and show the capabilities of standalone apps as this is like PCVR quality - top marks to the devs . I would love to see a multiplayer option for hanging around the campfire or sitting on the beach with friends and would love a media player for playing music or videos that everyone in the room can see together - one of the best experiences on the quest to date - well done to the devs

If I pay for a game all levels should be unlocked.  If we want to play a little games to unlock levels that give that option.  But as I said if you just want to experience this because you paid for it you shouldn't have to do all these ridiculous things to play extra levels.  

Side quests.......... That's what will really keep people interested 👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻👍🏻

reviews.review_title: I wish the scenes came unlocked
reviews.review_description: I understand finding the items unlocks each level but after a while I get frustrated and just want to progress to the next level. I wish I could bypass that some way. I just can’t find all the items therefore I can’t play the game I paid for. It goes from relaxing to frustrating.

This app is so visually and audibly pleasing. Very well done… EXCEPT for the task I’m forced to complete in order to enjoy the rest of it. 
I don’t like paying $150 to go to a fondue restaurant just cook my own food before I can enjoy eating it AND I certainly don’t like spending $10 just to be forced to find seashells in order to enjoy the rest of this immersive experience.  
Make the scavenger hunt an option. That’s my only complaint/suggestion.  
Warmest regards

Great, meditative environments with unobtrusive audio.  The only thing is, I cant find the last seashell and I’ve looked everywhere.  Wondering if I need to reset if it’s a glitch?

I’ve got the last two environments still locked, needing 1 seashell and 1 Saturn to unlock.  Assuming I’ll find the last Saturn in the seashell level but cant find the last shell!

I saw this game on YouTube and admired the beautiful graphics and the things I could do. I bought it, but honestly, it wasn't worth it. The graphics are very basic and not impressive at all. The gameplay is simply about walking around the world with very simple things you can do that aren't even worth mentioning. Currently, there are very few worlds, and they're all extremely small, mostly empty. I requested a refund, but they refused. Given the content and quality, the game should be free.

reviews.review_title: Very underwhelming and you can't refund
reviews.review_description: Well the YouTube shills got me again. The graphics are ok, nothing amazing compared to something like red matter 2. I got bored after 10min and requested a refund but you cannot refund this app.  I've been purchasing games since the CV1 days and although I rarely ask for a refund I've never been denied one.  If you don't have PCVR or PS VR2 and just want something to show your friends for 2 minutes this is not bad but otherwise save your money for something else.

This actually blew my mind. This is next to Jen VR. I thought I was on PCVR for a second but nope, this isn’t just some ordinary standalone slop game. It’s actually really good. The graphics are insane. There’s so many little tiny details that normal people would miss but I’m not normal. You can even indent the snow by putting your hand in it. You can even pick up snowballs you can even draw on the glass cause it’s foggy. This is insane. It has a total of seven worlds and all of them blew my mind. This is so cool. It’s definitely worth the $10 wow, just wow

First off the game has stunning visuals,  the tropical island just looks incredible.
This experience now has what everyone wanted
Smooth turn and faster walking, just sublime 
Congratulations on a great app.

I still can't get over the ocean effects.  You can actually walk in the water and actually see yourself sink into the ocean.. I  didn't get very far exploring the rest of the environments because I'm having a very hard time leaving the beach environment.  I love it.. feels so real moving around in the water like that.. . I'm having a ball here..

Looking forward to seeing more and more cool environments.  Congratulations to the brilliant app developers

The environments are really great from a tech point of view. The dark woods map, the snow map and the rain on the windows stood out for me.

One thing to maybe consider is the animations for the rain on the window and the trees in the night environment. The rain on the window looks a bit too big and sluggish, maybe reducing the size a bit of the rain drop might help. Also, the way the trees move in the night environment is good but sort of not realistic enough.

Also I don't like the photo backgrounds in the night environment and in others. It just makes it look flat. Maybe think about trying to edit it a bit to make it fuller like you have in the first environments with the clouds and stuff.

Lastly, you could maybe try to make mixed reality environments like you see with dreamscape. I find mixed reality environments to be more immersive. Maybe think about it for this app or another app in future.

Overall its a great app with a ton of cool environments so whay you have achieved here is excellent

I’ve been wanting something like this for a while. If you like peaceful environments and stuff that does a pretty good job with immersion, you should give this “game” a try. Just keep in mind it’s really more about walking around than anything else. I’m giving it 5 stars because it’s exactly my kind of thing. If the creator sees this, definitely keep adding little things here and there. Being able to virtually sit in a chair inside the environment would be great for immersion. There’s plenty of other stuff you could add too, but I really enjoyed what you've done.

PROS: Beautiful graphics + Interaction with some objects + Search for objects in order to unlock next landscapes
CONS: short (less than 1 hour)

Did not appreciate having to wander round like a twit looking at every smeggin rock just to find items to unlock experiences I thought payment unlocked. In fact, I'd rather payment DID unlocked them cos item hunting is sooooo boring. 

I'll keep it, cos, cool graphics, but unless I get lucky, I ain't seeing the later levels.

Also, it's madly overpriced for a tech demo.

update:
The island level now looks better. Another suggestion: it would be nice to also have the ability to swim—walking underwater as the basic locomotion, but if you move your hands appropriately, you could actually swim.

In my opinion, a priority would be improving hand posing on virtual objects. For example, when you grab a small stick, the fingers don’t properly conform to it—they clip through the object, and the same happens with other items.

In the future, you might also consider hand tracking for locomotion (as an alternative to the joystick when using hand tracking). In that case, you could implement running in place (using the user’s legs, like in the game HappyRun), or use the new built-in system that allows the player to move through thumb movement.

-------


This is a great experience, but it needs some improvements:

Improve hand interaction with virtual objects

Improve the graphics of the portals (maybe with a 3D effect)

Use the particle effect from the first portal (the small fading spheres that move along the floor) for the other portals as well

Add more enjoyable interactive objects

When you throw an object into the water from a certain distance, there is no “splash” effect (it would be better to add it)

I don’t see any fish in the island level
It would be better to add some, in places where they don’t cause FPS drops (ok in update there are fishs!)

Improve the underwater level:

Make the castle look older and covered with lichens, etc.

Improve the whale: it’s not realistic for it to stay still

Improve the corals (maybe reduce the color contrast?)

In general, try to make the underwater level more realistic


Try adding a bloom effect to the fireworks in the night level

Also in the night level, if possible, make the clouds move above the city

In the space level, the planet somehow doesn’t look impressive enough; maybe it needs stronger color contrast?

The toilet in the mountain cabin seems a bit out of scale (a bit too large)


I may have forgotten something, but everything else is absolutely wonderful—a really beautiful experience.

Of course, another way to improve it would be to add more environments.

reviews.review_title: nice idea but currently still imperfect
reviews.review_description:
The idea isn't bad; it’s a tool for relaxation. Some of the graphical elements are quite well-done, but from my perspective, a lot of it is still very incomplete.

The worlds aren't unlocked at the start; instead, the user has to search for items to unlock other worlds. It’s questionable whether you should have to do that in a relaxation experience, but let's say it’s okay for now. However, the developer didn't bother to implement a way to show the user which items the user has already found in which world. So, if you’re missing one seashell at the end, are you supposed to scour every single previous world again? What does that have to do with relaxation? Absolutely nothing! At the very least, it should show which world still has items to find; otherwise, you’re just wasting time in places you’ve already cleared. This is basic stuff that's just missing.

As for the worlds themselves: some are interesting, others aren't at all. 

There’s not much going on in the worlds, which is fine for relaxation, but the user can’t customize anything. You have to take what’s there, and it either works for you or it doesn't. Since the whole app is so limited in scope, I feel like the user should have some customization options. Take the 'house world' in the rain, for example: Why does the light in the house have to be on all the time? Why is there no light switch? Why can’t I change the time of day? How about some optional Music as an offer that user can turn on and off?

On top of that, some parts feel half-baked. Again, using the rain-house world as an example: I clip through the bed and the shelf. When I open the window and it's pouring rain outside, the sound inside doesn't change—same when I open the door. In a larger game, I’d call these flaws forgiveable, but because this app is so limited in scope and has so much less to do than a game, it just feels partially untested and unfinished.

reviews.review_title: The tropical island is my favourite
reviews.review_description: Can’t wait for new updates with more content and new locations. Your brain and imagination are magnificent. Please add more content soon

Just discovered this app on YouTube, as a VR developper myself I was impressed by the graphics so I bought it right away, was not disappointed. Really stunning graphics and physics (especially the water, the grass but also the sun and reflections). The environments are very beautiful, dreamy, and designed with care. It’s an indie app created to relax and push the visual boundaries of what is possible in VR, so don’t expect a big story or crazy puzzles to solve, but just what it says it does, a very relaxing and really stunning experience!

reviews.review_title: Really good, more like a tech demo
reviews.review_description:
Really cool to see into the future of what standalone vr can be like. 

Dev I asked voodoode to do a video on on solo developers. Hopefully he might. But I can't find a way to message you. If you can send an email or something please.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the review!
You can send me a message at specoolar@gmail.com

reviews.review_description:
can somebody help 
why are my hands turned upside down instead the right way up how can i fixe this problem 😞???

I really love the experience. Even though you can finish it quite quickly, it’s still impressive to see this level of quality on the Quest. So far, my favorite environments are the mountain cabin in the rain and the nighttime campfire.

I’d love to see more interactions added to each location, if possible. For example, in the first map, it would be great to have a boat that we can ride and paddle, along with different time settings like day and night, stars, or even a sunset.

For the green field map, adding more trees would make it feel richer. In the snow map, maybe include a small fire or more items like a bed to create the feeling of living in an igloo. And for the underwater map, it would be amazing to have the option to swim or float instead of walking, to make it more immersive and believable.

Overall, the experience is great, but it could really benefit from more elements in each map, maybe even some mini-games.

Also, if you could allow access to the Quest browser within these environments, so we can watch YouTube or Netflix while inside the maps, that would make the experience even more valuable.

One of my favorite Quest apps is Brink Traveler, and what you’ve created complements it really well. If something like your interactive mechanics could be combined with Brink’s real-world locations, it could become the ultimate VR travel experience. Imagine your water interactions in their Greece maps, or your campfire system in their forests, that would be incredible in VR.

Lots of fun. Potential for improvements and add-ons, but better than I expected. A nice place to wander around and fun to collect the items and interact with the surroundings. I
Even played with a beach ball in the ocean and knocked a fish out of the water. Also took a stroll over to see the wind turbines. Can’t wait to open more sceneries!

Just downloaded. Can’t wait to experience but all levels are locked. How do I unlock 1st level? Tried moving the tiny island, throwing at the portal, installing and reinstalling, nothing works.


There is no grip key? All I see is the palm plant on the podium and all portals are locked. I have moved the palm tree, swatted the light No key?  I don’t understand. Please help!

developer_response.body: Hi, just grabbing it with grip key should unlock it

reviews.review_description: Awesome graphics. My only criticism is that they should have made the last two maps easier to unlock. Or at least turn off the treasure hunt. BTW still can’t find all the pieces to unlock them.

Cannot complete unlocks because the shell is missing from the Windmill location.

(edit for response) There are still six required to unlock that environment, now only five can be found.🤦🏻‍♂️

developer_response.body:
I reduced the amount of shells required to unlock underwater scene in the last update. I removed that shell because that was hard to notice.

edit: I just speedrunned it and confirm you can find 6 shells. (In the previous version you needed to find 8). If you are struggling then DM me to specoolar@gmail.com, I will help.

reviews.review_description:
Conclusion: Worth your money and your time.

Thoughts: This is an atmospheric scavenger hunt, with a pace driven only by one's own curiosity and desire for exploration.

That hunt is an overlay on what is essentially a highly performant tech demo for how to create a compelling sense of place using the current-gen mobile hardware capabilities. The featured places are absolutely worth visiting, letting that atmosphere wash over you, and grounding yourself in the experience.

I've seen a lot of "here's a cool place to visit" apps over the years, and this is among the very best (and much more engaging).

After all the hype  about the graphics I'm rather disappointed with the app. Yes, the graphics are good for Quest standards, but they're also very sterile and lack content; there's not much to do besides collecting useless things. What I find annoying is that the app can't be used while lying down, as you practically sink into the grass because there's no height adjustment.  Disappointing for an app that is considered to be a relaxation app

I really like these worlds. They as others have said very peaceful and atmospheric. 
Dev's is it possible you could add functions in worlds, where  you could make for example the beautiful island have a function where you can change it to sunset .🙏
Then have the beautiful colors of a sunset to relax in also.
Simple but awesome if you like peaceful meditative spaces to explore.
Very beautiful and feels like a small getaway. Water is amazing! It reacts like water should and the best I have seen on the Quest. Hoping more is added soon!

truly, one of the most wonderful games out there I do say it is quite short. I was able to finish the entire game in less than an hour, but I do say it is absolutely stunning. The best experience I’ve ever had. They need to make a part two ￼
It's absolutely incredible, but for 5 stars in my opinion, it needs smooth turning; turning at angles completely ruins the beauty of this environment.
Thanks to all the VR game devs who actually use the Quest three's capabilities to the fullest. Thanks for actually spending time on your game and optimizing it for a great experience.

So far I am liking what I see here as it is in next a very beautiful backgrounds. I am a little frustrated because I cannot pick up the seashell under the water or the planet in the shock because I can't bend down and reach them. Any ideas?

developer_response.body:
Hi, 
In the new version I added a feature that lets you crouch virtually by pressing button B.
Thank you!

reviews.review_description:
Great little "game" to show people something simple and good looking in vr.
Needs the ability to know what ornaments are left in each level so we dont waste our time.
I pay for it.. but I can't even unlock the scenes.  Don't waste your money.  Plus it will make you motion sick and you won't be able to get a refund.

developer_response.body:
The description literally says you collect objects to unlock scenes.
You can use teleporting if smooth motion is uncomfortable for you.

reviews.review_description: this is impressive work. Beautiful and immersive, smart attention to details, collision detection minimization and effective use of foveated rendering. I believe this is essential experience, will watch this developer
It's worth it just for having your own private beach to relax on. And you get the added bonus of being able to interact with the water and other things laying around.
Had a lot of fun making a video on this game and seeing just how much you can push the quest hardware. This game is a true hidden gem that everyone needs to see!
A great place to relax and enjoy as you find objects to unlock the next destination. Also great graphics.
Can't wait for more, not a game but nice environments,
Reality meets a VR experience. Truth be told, if you're looking to take a vacation or something THIS IS IT‼️
Can’t reach anything on the ground, needs hight adjustment or I can’t unlock the last two levels.
A good experience to make people approach VR. I hope they'll add more scenes

reviews.review_title: Wish I could experience it agian
reviews.review_description: I rarely ever write reviews, but I felt absolutely compelled to for this app. To be honest, I had really low expectations going in, VR graphics on Meta usually disappoint me. However, I was completely stunned the moment I opened the first "world." This experience blew right past my expectations, and the fireworks by the campfire honestly brought me close to tears. If you are going through a hard time, need a peaceful escape from a busy life, or just want to be alone in some breathtaking, mystical worlds, please get this app. I kept losing myself in each world, yet was still so excited for the next one to wow me. My only wish is that I could forget everything and replay this for the first time, so do me a favor and do that for me. Thank you developers.

developer_response.body: This made my day🥹. Thank you very much!

reviews.review_description: It'd be nice when you rub your hands across the blades of grass it bends with your hands need more things and environment The grass level need more things going on more buildings or something a pool some balloons stuff to do in environment that'd be cool The space ones need more things to do in environment besides pushing space rocks but you can drive around space walk drive a hover car punch some aliens or see some aliens running around something to do on space and the house with the rain that'd be cool if you can walk out with umbrella I'll get those stuff out the window something to do and then each environment something to do in the water more creatures and water like squids more sharks dolphins seals lobsters crabs shrimps octopus shoot and ink at you and you got too close or squid my bag squid stingrays add some animals people will be nice all that that part with the firewood when you on the mountain that we could have more stuff to do up there like roast some marshmallows cook some food a little grill you can feel like you're cooking food Play a little music on the radio Wave that the guy that's down at the other end he if you wave at them he'll wave back at you like you know you there and say how are you doing at more things like mountain climbing a kayak riding in the water down the stream or creek
Sweeet

reviews.review_title: Amazing game to explore; word to devs
reviews.review_description: This game gave me an amazing first impression to vr and I get it is cool and real impressive for a quest 3 game but sometimes the little details matter to like more stuff you can mess around with and the most important thing functioning￼ toilets ￼those kinda little details, also I hope there will be more levels in future updates and I can’t say this enough this game is truly a masterpiece I wish you luck on your dev journey!

reviews.review_title: Lucent is a great game in my opinion 😛
reviews.review_description: I loved this game and pls add more, the water and exploration of this place Was really peaceful I wish if u could add more activities or things to this w games it’s soooooooo amazing because of it not being a pic game. :)

So first off this has no replay ability so once u unlock the environments that's it .. this is definitely more of a relaxing and show your friends what's this headset is capable of doing if the dev knows what they doing and this dev definitely 🙌💯💪🏽 knocked this one out the park these other devs out there need to learn from this one he has the best atmospheric feels and performance than most stand alone apps I thought I was on pcvr once I changed scale to 1.4 and anti alising to 2x it was soo clear I like u can do this option without quest game optimizer.... definitely worth 10$ for the experience I can just sit in there and relax i just hope u add more environments 🙌 ...... only thing I wanted to see was smooth turn option and maybe body figure and the ocean underwater part to be able to swim underwater instead of walking on floor..... keep up the good work 💯💪🏽

With this one you just set the tone for Quest 3 graphics and sounds... well done...

2 things... first, the sound when you collect an object is not that good, kicked you out of immersion.. second... it would be nice if you can walk around the asteroid, an easy way to get another invinity level... well done

reviews.review_title: Big fan, wish for 3 small differences
reviews.review_description:
I found this through the review in upload VR. This is exactly the sort of thing I really enjoy VR for the sense of space and a place to get away without a bunch of puzzles or stress to worry about. Looks and sounds great. My favorite area is the rainy little house with the window pulled open.

One mild concern was how long it would take to unlock the other areas - including the ones I was most interested in. I was able to easily find all the hidden objects in my first sitting, it felt about right and I didn't mind it giving me a reason to move around.

Speaking of moving, my only small requests that I wish were a little different is first, the maximum move speed still feels very slow compared to other experiences I'm used to. so I resorted to rapidly pumping the teleport button to walk faster when looking for the original objects.

Secondly, I really enjoy the cloud effect. I've been shooting cloud photography and time lapses for over ten years, it gets edge movement right in a way many skyboxes don't even attempt. However it changes far too rapidly, so instead of looking like clouds, it comes across as a painterly decorative effect. Others may disagree but personally I feel the clouds would look much more convincing if the speed of the cloud movement effect was greatly slowed down.

Lastly, when the Quest UI is brought up to access the browser etc the whole scene hides. This prevents using it as a workspace while browsing or using remote desktop. I enjoy that functionality from some other apps, but perhaps for performance reasons this was necessary to hide the scene here when it's paused? I could spend more hours here if I were able to use the spaces as a background while I do other things in the quest interface windows.

I was happy to see the notice in-game that no generative AI was used! Thank you for making this.

developer_response.body:
Hi,
I just updated. Now you should be able to increase move speed in the options in the menu scene.
Also I removed the scene hiding behaviour when switching to another app. (Initially it was a designing mistake).
Thank you!

reviews.review_description:
.. love it, keeping it. but can you please add a point to grab option.
i mostly play these kind of apps sitting on a bed, therefore can’t reach stuff thats beyond my hips- matress.. thanks

This is amazing. It's so nice to do absolutely nothing in a beautiful environment. Great physics for a standalone game as well. As a beach, space and nature enjoyer, this is the best game to play after a long day of school or work. Definitely update the game, add new locations. I reckon adding a rainy cyberpunk type city outside and inside. I know it doesn't fit with the vibe of the game, but cmon. Would definitely recommend if you just want to relax. 10/10, would get faded here again.
This is breathtaking never seen water effects like this a totally must buy
Fantastic app. Very relaxing.
You should definitely add slime that you can play with and jello
The app to put newbies into to open their eyes to the wonder of Virtual Reality.
This is an amazing experience.
Really fun little experience with great visuals.
Also good for a bit of meditative contemplation
SMOOTH TURN OPTION, PLS!!!

developer_response.body:
Thank you!
I am working on smooth turning in the new update

reviews.review_description: Like the graphics looks great
Really well done
